assignments.name: policy
assignments.value: Ollama only parses/reasons. Nutrition values must come only from official BLV API. Never add manual nutrient values.

assignments.name: prompt
assignments.value:
={ `AI-Workflow-Nutrition v8. Return strict JSON only.

LIFECYCLE STEP: Design Thinking.
Tasks:
1. Create a clear global product vision for a nutrition analysis app.
2. Generate realistic Azure DevOps-style user stories.
3. For each user story include: id, title, role, need, value, acceptance_criteria, priority, dependencies, technical_notes.
4. Prepare an azure_payload array that could be sent to Azure DevOps work items.

Hard rules:
- Ollama must never generate nutrition values.
- Nutrition values must come only from official BLV API.
- Do not invent BLV endpoints.

Return shape:
{"vision":{"problem":"","target_users":[],"goals":[],"success_metrics":[]},"user_stories":[{"id":"US-01","title":"","role":"","need":"","value":"","priority":"high|medium|low","acceptance_criteria":[],"dependencies":[],"technical_notes":[]}],"azure_payload":[{"op":"add","path":"/fields/System.Title","value":""}]}
` }

assignments.name: prompt
assignments.value:
={ `AI-Workflow-Nutrition v8. Return strict JSON only.

LIFECYCLE STEP: Production / Global architecture.
Use the previous Design Thinking output and user stories available in the JSON context.

Tasks:
1. Build a global architecture from all user stories.
2. Define modules: input parser, quantity normalizer, BLV search, BLV detail, candidate review, nutrition calculator, API server, UI, testing, security, deployment, maintenance.
3. Map each user story to process/frontend/backend/test/security tasks.
4. Keep the architecture compatible with local n8n + local Ollama + Node/Express app.

Hard rules:
- Ollama is only for parsing/reasoning.
- All nutrition data must come from official BLV API.
- Do not invent endpoints.

Return shape:
{"architecture":{"overview":"","modules":[],"data_flow":[],"constraints":[]},"user_story_task_map":[{"story_id":"US-01","process":[],"frontend":[],"backend":[],"tests":[],"security":[]}]}
` }

assignments.name: prompt
assignments.value:
={ `AI-Workflow-Nutrition v8. Return strict JSON only.

LIFECYCLE STEP: Production by user story.
Use the previous user stories and architecture from JSON context.

For each user story:
1. Retrieve the user story intent.
2. Generate process logic.
3. Generate frontend requirements.
4. Generate backend requirements.
5. Generate test requirements.
6. Explain which files should be created/modified.

Also produce one merged implementation plan for the generated app source.

Hard rules:
- Do not manually create nutrition values.
- Nutrients must be computed only from official BLV response values.
- Preserve BLV cache, human review mode, generated .env, pretty UI, and retry guard.

Return shape:
{"production_by_user_story":[{"story_id":"US-01","process":{},"frontend":{},"backend":{},"tests":{},"files":[]}],"merged_app_plan":{"files":[],"runtime_flow":[],"quality_gates":[]}}
` }

assignments.name: prompt
assignments.value:
={{ `You are the AI-Workflow-Nutrition CODE FIX AGENT.

The app source was generated by Ollama and failed validation. n8n is only the centralized orchestrator; you must generate the corrected file contents.

Current global fix attempt: ${$json.fix_attempt}. Max attempts: ${$json.max_fix_attempts}.

HARD RULES:
- Return strict JSON only. No markdown.
- NEVER add fixed nutrition values.
- NEVER invent calories, proteins, fat, carbohydrates or micronutrients.
- Nutrition totals must be derived only from official_nutrients returned by BLV.
- Preserve official BLV API only policy.
- Preserve cache and human review behavior.
- If files are missing, generate the missing files.
- If a file is broken, return its full corrected content.

Existing files:
${JSON.stringify(Object.keys($json.generated_app_source || {}), null, 2)}

Failures:
${JSON.stringify($json.runtime_quality_failures || [], null, 2)}

Warnings:
${JSON.stringify($json.runtime_quality_warnings || [], null, 2)}

HTTP app result if available:
${JSON.stringify($json.runtime_http_result || {}, null, 2)}

Return shape:
{
  "status": "fix_files",
  "diagnosis": ["string"],
  "files_to_replace": [
    {"path": "app/src/example.ts", "content": "full corrected file content"}
  ],
  "tests_to_rerun": ["static_source_quality", "running_app_http_validation"],
  "safety_checks": ["no manual nutrition values", "official BLV only"]
}
` }}

assignments.name: prompt
assignments.value:
={ `AI-Workflow-Nutrition v8. Return strict JSON only.

LIFECYCLE STEP: Test / improvement.
Use the generated user stories, production plan, runtime quality results, and user_story_conformity_report.

Tasks:
1. Check whether each user story is covered by generated process/frontend/backend/tests.
2. Identify gaps and propose improvements.
3. Distinguish blocking issues from warnings.
4. Do not trigger code correction for environment-only issues such as app unreachable or BLV returning no usable candidate.

Hard rules:
- No invented nutrition values.
- Official BLV API only.

Return shape:
{"test_status":"pass|warning|fail","user_story_checks":[{"story_id":"US-01","status":"pass|warning|fail","gaps":[],"improvements":[]}],"global_improvements":[],"blocking_issues":[],"non_blocking_warnings":[]}
` }

assignments.name: prompt
assignments.value:
={ `AI-Workflow-Nutrition v8. Return strict JSON only.

LIFECYCLE STEP: Security.
Analyze the generated project and workflow.

Tasks:
1. Identify security/privacy risks for local n8n, local Ollama, Express app, BLV API calls, logs, .env, user meal data.
2. Propose mitigations.
3. Create a security improvement backlog.
4. Keep local-first architecture and avoid cloud LLM costs.

Hard rules:
- Never suggest storing sensitive meal data unnecessarily.
- Never suggest exposing Ollama publicly.
- Never suggest invented nutrition data.

Return shape:
{"security_status":"pass|warning|fail","findings":[{"severity":"low|medium|high","area":"","risk":"","mitigation":""}],"security_backlog":[],"ready_for_local_mvp":true}
` }

assignments.name: prompt
assignments.value:
={ `AI-Workflow-Nutrition v8. Return strict JSON only.

LIFECYCLE STEP: Mise en route / maintenance.
Use the generated app, reports, security review, and runtime validation state.

Tasks:
1. Generate local deployment plan for Docker n8n + local Ollama + generated Node app.
2. Generate startup commands.
3. Generate troubleshooting steps.
4. Generate maintenance/self-healing rules: detect issue, classify code issue vs environment issue, retry max 10 only for code issues, otherwise human review.
5. Do not invent versions.

Return shape:
{"deployment":{"steps":[],"commands":[],"health_checks":[]},"maintenance":{"self_healing_rules":[],"max_auto_fixes":10,"human_review_triggers":[]},"known_environment_issues":[],"ready_to_run":true}
` }

nodes.name: Prepare Azure DevOps User Stories Payload

parameters.content:
# AI-Workflow-Nutrition — Workflow v9.1 documenté — code généré par Ollama

Objectif : générer une application d'analyse nutritionnelle prête à lancer, en passant par un cycle complet :
Design Thinking → Architecture → Production → Génération de code Ollama en plusieurs lots → Tests → Corrections → Sécurité → Déploiement → ZIP final.

Règle centrale : Ollama aide à raisonner et parser, mais les valeurs nutritionnelles doivent venir uniquement de l'API officielle BLV.

## 01 — Initialisation globale

Lance le workflow, définit le contexte global : projet, Ollama, modèle, API BLV, limite de corrections, règles principales.

- `Manual Trigger` lance le workflow à la main.
- `Set Global Context` pose les constantes du projet :
  nom du projet, URL Ollama, modèle local, API BLV, endpoints BLV, limite max de corrections.
- `Reset Runtime Loop State` remet à zéro l'état global de la boucle qualité.

Cette zone évite que les anciennes exécutions polluent les nouveaux tests.

## 02 — Design Thinking + User Stories

Demande à Ollama de générer la vision produit, les user stories et un payload simulé Azure DevOps.

- Génère la vision produit.
- Crée les user stories.
- Prépare un payload simulé compatible Azure DevOps.

But : partir d'un besoin métier clair avant de produire du code.

## 03 — Architecture globale

Demande à Ollama de transformer les user stories en architecture technique : modules, data flow, frontend, backend, tests, sécurité.

Ollama transforme les user stories en architecture technique :
- modules frontend / backend / tests / sécurité,
- data flow complet,
- contraintes locales : n8n + Ollama + Node/Express.

Cette étape sert de plan directeur pour la génération.

## 04 — Production de l'application

- Génère le plan de production, puis déclenche la génération de code par Ollama.
- Décompose chaque user story en process, front, back, tests et fichiers.
- `Call Ollama Code Generation` demande à Ollama de générer les fichiers TypeScript / HTML / README / .env.

Le workflow garde le contexte, mais le code final de l’app est produit par Ollama puis validé par n8n.

##  Règle non négociable

Ollama ne doit jamais inventer :
- calories,
- protéines,
- lipides,
- glucides,
- micronutriments.

Les nutriments viennent uniquement de `official_nutrients` récupéré via BLV.
Si la donnée manque ou si le match est ambigu : warning ou human review.

## 05 — Runtime Quality Gates

Vérifie statiquement les fichiers générés : fichiers obligatoires, .env, cache BLV, human review, appels BLV, calculs nutritionnels.

Le workflow contrôle la qualité avant d'aller plus loin :
- tests statiques sur les fichiers générés,
- vérification des endpoints BLV,
- présence du cache BLV,
- présence du mode human review,
- contrôle des calculs de macros depuis les codes BLV.

Si ça passe, le workflow continue. Sinon, il entre en boucle de correction.

## 06 — Test HTTP réel

Appelle réellement l’endpoint /analyze de l’application générée pour vérifier son comportement.

Cette zone appelle l'application générée sur `/analyze`.

Elle vérifie :
- que les aliments sont détectés,
- que les quantités sont normalisées,
- que les totals calories/protéines/lipides/glucides sont numériques quand BLV retourne des nutriments,
- que la provenance reste `official BLV API only`.

Si l'app locale n'est pas lancée, c'est classé comme problème d'environnement.

## 07 — Collapse des validations

Regroupe les résultats de validation en un seul rapport global pour éviter plusieurs corrections en parallèle

Pourquoi ?
- éviter 10 corrections en parallèle,
- garder une seule décision claire,
- éviter que le fix agent corrige plusieurs fois le même problème.

## 08 — Boucle de correction sécurisée

`Global Runtime Fix Attempt Guard` décide si une correction automatique est autorisée.

Règles :
- maximum 10 vraies corrections code,
- pas de retry si l'erreur vient seulement de l'environnement,
- arrêt en human review si la limite est atteinte,
- un seul appel global au fix agent par tentative.

## 09 — Runtime Fix Agent

Renvoie les erreurs à Ollama pour qu’il génère les fichiers corrigés, sans inventer de valeurs nutritionnelles.

Le fix agent reçoit :
- les failures globales,
- le résultat HTTP si disponible,
- les règles de sécurité.

Il doit proposer une correction minimale.
Il n'a pas le droit d'ajouter des valeurs nutritionnelles fixes.
`Apply Safe Runtime Patch` applique seulement des patchs contrôlés.

## 10 — Human Review

Arrête la correction automatique si le problème est ambigu, externe, environnemental ou si la limite est atteinte.

Si la correction auto n'est pas adaptée :
- max 10 atteint,
- app non lancée,
- problème externe BLV,
- ambiguïté trop forte,

alors le workflow produit un rapport `runtime-human-review-required.txt`.
Le workflow reste sûr au lieu de forcer une mauvaise correction.

## 11 — Test / amélioration

Vérifie si les user stories sont couvertes et propose des améliorations globales.

Cette étape vérifie si les user stories sont bien couvertes :
- process,
- frontend,
- backend,
- tests,
- conformité globale.

Elle propose des améliorations sans forcément modifier le code.

## 12 — Sécurité

Analyse les risques :
- données repas utilisateur,
- exposition d'Ollama,
- configuration `.env`,
- appels API,
- logs et erreurs.

Génère ensuite un backlog d'améliorations sécurité pour le projet.

## 13 — Déploiement / Maintenance

Produit :
- les étapes de lancement local,
- les commandes utiles,
- les health checks,
- les règles de self-healing.

Important : le workflow distingue problème de code et problème d'environnement.

## 14 — Génération du package final

Cette zone fabrique :
- `app-ready-to-run.zip`,
- le rapport final lifecycle,
- le manifest du ZIP,
- les fichiers lifecycle / sécurité / maintenance.

`Validate Final ZIP Contents` vérifie que tous les fichiers essentiels sont présents avant le téléchargement.

## Sortie finale

`FINAL - Download App ZIP` retourne le ZIP prêt à utiliser.

Utilisation :
1. Télécharger le ZIP
2. Extraire
3. `cd app`
4. `npm install`
5. `npm run dev`

Puis ouvrir `http://localhost:3000`.

parameters.jsCode:
const groupName = "config and project setup";
const groupRequiredFiles = ["app/package.json", "app/tsconfig.json", "app/.env.example", "app/.env", "app/README.md"];
const groupRequirements = ["package.json must include scripts dev, build and start. Prefer tsx for dev and TypeScript compiler for build.", "tsconfig must support NodeNext modules and strict TypeScript.", ".env.example and .env must include PORT, OLLAMA_URL, OLLAMA_MODEL, BLV_API_BASE_URL, BLV_SEARCH_PATH=foods, BLV_DETAIL_PATH=food/:id, BLV_CACHE_ENABLED=true, HUMAN_REVIEW thresholds.", "README must explain npm install and npm run dev."];
const context = {
  project_name: $json.project_name || 'AI-Workflow-Nutrition',
  policy: $json.policy || 'Nutrition values must come only from official BLV API. Never add manual nutrient values.',
  blv_api_base_url: $json.blv_api_base_url || 'https://api.webapp.prod.blv.foodcase-services.com/BLV_WebApp_WS/webresources/BLV-api',
  blv_search_path: $json.blv_search_path || 'foods',
  blv_detail_path: $json.blv_detail_path || 'food/:id',
  user_stories: $json.user_stories || [],
  architecture: $json.architecture || {},
  production: $json.production || {},
  generated_user_story_outputs: $json.generated_user_story_outputs || [],
  existing_generated_app_source_files: Object.keys($json.generated_app_source || {})
};
const prompt = `You are Ollama, the CODE GENERATION AGENT for AI-Workflow-Nutrition.
Return STRICT JSON ONLY. No markdown. No prose outside JSON.

IMPORTANT
n8n is only the centralized orchestrator. The application code must be generated by you, Ollama.
Generate ONLY this file group: ${groupName}.

PROJECT CONTEXT
${JSON.stringify(context, null, 2)}

FILES TO GENERATE IN THIS CALL
${groupRequiredFiles.map(f => '- ' + f).join('\n')}

GROUP REQUIREMENTS
${groupRequirements.map(r => '- ' + r).join('\n')}

GLOBAL APP RULES
- Node/Express + TypeScript app.
- Use Express, cors, dotenv and native fetch.
- GET /health must return JSON status ok.
- POST /analyze must accept {"raw_meal_input":"..."}.
- Ollama may parse food names/quantities/units but must NEVER generate nutrition values.
- All calories, proteins, fat, carbohydrates and micronutrients must come only from the official Swiss BLV API response.
- BLV config: BLV_SEARCH_PATH=foods and BLV_DETAIL_PATH=food/:id.
- BLVClient must use query parameter named "search".
- Include cache and human review when candidates are missing or ambiguous.
- NutritionCalculator must derive totals from official_nutrients only and support ENERCC, PROT625, FAT, CHO plus common fallback codes/names.
- Do not hardcode nutrition values anywhere.

REQUIRED JSON OUTPUT SHAPE
{
  "generation_metadata": {
    "generated_by": "ollama",
    "group": "${groupName}",
    "notes": []
  },
  "files": [
    {"path":"app/example.ts","content":"full file content"}
  ]
}

JSON RULES
- Return valid JSON parseable by JSON.parse.
- Escape newlines inside content strings.
- Do not use markdown code fences.
- Include every requested file exactly once.
- Do not include files from other groups.
`;
return [{ json: { ...$json, prompt, ollama_generation_group: groupName, group_required_files: groupRequiredFiles, ollama_group_position: "Build Ollama Config Files Prompt" } }];

const groupName = "domain parser normalizer and BLV client";
const groupRequiredFiles = ["app/src/domain/NormalizedFood.ts", "app/src/domain/NutritionTotals.ts", "app/src/services/OllamaMealParser.ts", "app/src/services/QuantityNormalizer.ts", "app/src/services/BLVClient.ts"];
const groupRequirements = ["NormalizedFood and NutritionTotals must be domain classes or typed exports.", "OllamaMealParser must call local Ollama for parsing only and include deterministic fallback parsing.", "QuantityNormalizer must convert grams/kg and estimate simple units deterministically.", "BLVClient must implement searchFood, getNutrients, cacheStats and use search/detail cache.", "BLVClient searchFood must use URL search parameter named search."];
const context = {
  project_name: $json.project_name || 'AI-Workflow-Nutrition',

};

`;
return [{ json: { ...$json, prompt, ollama_generation_group: groupName, group_required_files: groupRequiredFiles, ollama_group_position: "Build Ollama Frontend Prompt" } }];

parameters.content:
04 - Production par user story	Transforme les user stories en plan de production : process, frontend, backend, tests et fichiers à générer.

Génération code Ollama - Config files	Ollama génère les fichiers de configuration : package.json, tsconfig.json, .env, .env.example, README.

Génération code Ollama - Parser / BLV files	Ollama génère les fichiers liés au parsing, à la normalisation des quantités et au client BLV.

Génération code Ollama - App logic files	Ollama génère la logique principale : review des candidats, calcul nutritionnel, orchestration de l’app et serveur Express.

Génération code Ollama - Frontend files	Ollama génère l’interface web, notamment index.html.
Parse des réponses Ollama	Convertit les réponses brutes d’Ollama en vrais fichiers exploitables par n8n.

Merge Ollama Generated App Source	Fusionne tous les fichiers générés par Ollama dans un seul objet generated_app_source

main.node: Prepare Azure DevOps User Stories Payload
